column1: # Contact description for the assembly statics problem.
# Column definitions:
#   body_A      : first  body at this contact (0 = ground)
#   body_B      : second body at this contact (0 = ground)
#   x           : x-coordinate of the contact point [mm]
#   y           : y-coordinate of the contact point [mm]
#   normal_deg  : angle of the inward contact normal INTO body_A [degrees
#                 measured CCW from the positive x-axis]
#   mu          : coefficient of friction at this contact [-]
# Physical scenario:
#   Row 1 — Body 1 (diagonal bar) rests on the ground at its lower-left corner.
#            The ground pushes Body 1 upward → normal into Body 1 = 90°.
#            Friction must balance the horizontal push from Body 2 (ratio 1/3 < 0.50 ✓).
#   Row 2 — Body 1 presses its upper-right corner against Body 2's left face.
#            Body 2 pushes Body 1 to the left → normal into Body 1 = 180°.
#            (Reaction: Body 1 pushes Body 2 to the right
#             which is encoded implicitly as the opposite of this contact's normal.)
#   Row 3 — Body 2 (vertical block) rests on the ground at its left-bottom corner.
#            The ground pushes Body 2 upward → normal into Body 2 = 90°.
#            Friction at this contact is zero in equilibrium (assumed here).
#   Row 4 — Body 2 rests on the ground at its right-bottom corner.
#            The ground pushes Body 2 upward → normal into Body 2 = 90°.
#            Friction here balances the rightward push transmitted from Body 1
body_A
